title: Define each metric once in a governed layer
description: Encode business metrics in a single metrics layer so every dashboard and query derives the same number from one definition.
body: Use when the same metric (revenue, active users) is consumed by multiple tools or teams. Do not let each dashboard re-implement the calculation in its own SQL — definitions drift and reported numbers disagree. Actions: define the metric, its grain, and its filters once in a metrics layer (LookML, Cube, MetricFlow), and have every consumer query through it. Validation signals include the same metric returning identical values across two consuming tools and a single source-of-truth definition under version control. Risk boundary: divergent metric definitions erode trust in the warehouse; if two surfaces report different numbers for one metric, treat it as a governance defect and consolidate before publishing.
